name: judicial_technology_port_security_kestrel_jus_mer
premise: Two departments published incompatible guidance; frontline staff are improvising and liability is concentrating on one signature line. In Judicial Technology, the situation tagged internally around judicial technology port security kestrel jus mer has converged with local pressure in the Port corridor. That label is not a full explanation, but it is how crews, counsel, and comms are indexing the case file right now. Finance notes that the fastest mitigation spends down a restricted reserve that another program legally depends on next quarter. Middle management filtered an escalation; the executive on point is hearing some details for the first time on this bridge. A public briefing is scheduled; if no coherent position exists, staff will improvise talking points from conflicting drafts. Framing has already hardened externally as justice against mercy; internal nuance will not survive the first news cycle unless it is extremely careful.

agents.name: Sana Diallo
agents.identity: I am Sana Diallo, the duty officer with sign-off authority for the next window in Judicial Technology. I own the signature line that turns a recommendation into an irreversible operational fact for the next phase.
agents.goal: I need a path that does not treat mercy as disposable collateral every time justice spikes in urgency, but I also cannot pretend we have luxury time we do not have.

agents.name: Camila Haddad
agents.identity: I am Camila Haddad, the advocate representing the population most likely to lose if shortcuts normalize in Judicial Technology. I am tracking who gets protected last if we choose speed, silence, or a narrow definition of success.
agents.goal: I want explicit guardrails so justice cannot become a blank check that quietly vaporizes mercy in the paperwork afterward.

choices: I will sequence a conservative rollout: smallest blast radius first, documented exceptions only, and real appeals paths—even if justice advocates call that foot-dragging.
I will push a fast, organization-wide lever because partial fixes may leave the worst harm untouched—even if that compresses protections for mercy.
forced_choice.call_to_action: Two incompatible drafts are circulating; only one can become official. Sana Diallo must choose under justice versus mercy. What does Sana Diallo do?
